title: compose_warning_logs is not respected
body:
### Issue Description

Hi! In the latest 5.2.2 release, the `compose_warning_logs` config value along with its `PODMAN_COMPOSE_WARNING_LOGS` env var are not respected and the warning message still appears

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run `podman compose X`

### Describe the results you received

The warning message is displayed

### Describe the results you expected

No warning message

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

comments.body:
compose_warning_logs from containers.conf already works on 5.2.2

The env var was added in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23442 and will be released with 5.3

> compose_warning_logs from containers.conf already works on 5.2.2
> 
> The env var was added in #23442 and will be released with 5.3

Turns out I didn't add the option under the right section actually sorry for that!

To whoever is struggling with the `containers.conf` solution:

1. Create the file `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`;
2. Add the following content:

```
# Emit logs on each invocation of the compose command indicating that an
# external compose provider is being executed.
[engine]
compose_warning_logs=false
```
